title: feat: Stripe payment processor integration
body:
## Summary

Integrates Stripe as a full payment processor alongside the existing PayPal integration. This is the foundation for the payment modernization work in PLAT-2001.

## Changes

### New files
- `src/payments/stripe-client.ts` — singleton Stripe client with idempotency key utils
- `src/api/v2/payments/checkout.ts` — POST /api/v2/payments/checkout creates Stripe Checkout Sessions
- `src/api/v2/webhooks/stripe.ts` — handles `checkout.session.completed`, `payment_intent.*`, `customer.subscription.*` events with idempotent deduplication
- `src/payments/subscriptions.ts` — createSubscription, cancelSubscription wrapping Stripe Subscriptions API
- `migrations/20240319_001_payment_multiprocessor.sql` — **BREAKING** schema migration

### Breaking change
The `payments` and `subscriptions` tables are restructured to support multiple processors. See migration SQL.
- `Subscription.paypalSubscriptionId` → `externalSubscriptionId`
- `Subscription.processor` column added (required)
- `Payment.processor` column added (required, backfill = 'paypal')

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests for stripe-client, checkout, webhook handler
- [x] Integration test with Stripe test mode against staging database
- [x] Manual end-to-end: checkout → Stripe webhook → subscription active
- [x] Rollback tested: migration down script verified

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2002
- Jira: PLAT-2003
- Depends on: PLAT-2001 (Epic)

title: feat: payment history API with cursor-based pagination
body:
## Summary

Adds `GET /api/v2/payments/history` endpoint for retrieving paginated payment records.

## Changes
- `src/api/v2/payments/history.ts` — new endpoint
- Supports cursor-based pagination via `after` query param
- Filter by status, processor, and date range
- Wired into payments router

## API
```
GET /api/v2/payments/history?limit=20&after=<cursor>&status=succeeded&processor=stripe

Response:
{
  data: Payment[],
  pagination: { hasMore: boolean, nextCursor: string | null }
}
```

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests for pagination logic
- [x] Manual testing with both stripe and paypal records

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2004

title: feat: real-time WebSocket notification service
body:
## Summary

Adds a WebSocket-based notification service so users receive live events without polling.

## Architecture
- Server-side: `ws` library WebSocket server mounted at `/ws`, attached to the existing HTTP server
- Authentication: JWT extracted from WS upgrade request `?token=` query param
- Connection registry: in-memory `Map<userId, Set<WebSocket>>` for targeted delivery
- Heartbeat: 30s ping/pong to detect and terminate stale connections

## Notification event types
- `payment_succeeded` / `payment_failed`
- `subscription_renewed` / `subscription_canceled`
- `invoice_generated`
- `security_alert`

## Client SDK
`src/client/notifications-sdk.ts` — `NotificationsClient` class with jittered exponential backoff reconnect (max 30s). Compatible with React via EventEmitter interface.

## Changes
- `src/notifications/ws-server.ts`
- `src/notifications/types.ts`
- `src/notifications/handlers.ts`
- `src/client/notifications-sdk.ts`
- `src/server.ts` — attach WS server

## Test plan
- [x] Unit: connection auth, heartbeat, deregister on close
- [x] Unit: delivery to multi-connection users
- [x] Integration: send notification, assert client receives within 200ms
- [x] Load test: 1000 concurrent connections on staging — no memory leak after 30min

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2010

title: perf: database query optimizations for dashboard and reporting
body:
## Summary

Fixes the root cause of PLAT-2015 (dashboard loading >10s for large orgs) and adds preventative indexes from PLAT-2016.

## Root Cause
The `/api/v2/dashboard/summary` endpoint was executing 1 + N queries (N = org member count). For orgs with 1000+ users this caused 1000+ individual `COUNT(*)` queries, taking 10–20 seconds total.

## Fix (PLAT-2015)
Replaced N+1 loop with a single raw SQL CTE that aggregates all counts in one pass. Query time in staging: **12,000ms → 95ms** for a 1,200-member org.

## Indexes (PLAT-2016)
Added composite indexes on `user_events` and `org_reports` tables:
- `idx_user_events_org_created` — for 30-day time-range dashboard queries
- `idx_user_events_org_type_created` — for event-type analytics
- `idx_org_reports_org_period` — for billing period reports
- `idx_payments_org_processor_status` — for billing dashboard filters

All created with `CONCURRENTLY` to avoid locking production tables.

## Test plan
- [x] Verified query plan with EXPLAIN ANALYZE in staging (seq scan → index scan)
- [x] Load test: 50 concurrent dashboard requests, p99 < 200ms
- [x] No regression on orgs with <10 members

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2015
- Jira: PLAT-2016

title: feat+fix: API rate limiting and critical security patches
body:
## Summary

Combines two related security/stability improvements:
1. **Rate limiting** (PLAT-2020): prevents API abuse
2. **SQL injection fix** (PLAT-2025): critical CVE patch

⚠️ **This PR includes a CRITICAL security fix. Review and merge priority is HIGH.**

## Rate Limiting (PLAT-2020)
Token-bucket rate limiter middleware with Redis backing and in-memory LRU fallback.

Limits:
- Global authenticated: 300 req/min per org
- Unauthenticated: 30 req/min per IP  
- Search endpoint: 60 req/min per org
- Stripe webhooks: 500 req/min (generous for retries)

Returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header.

## SQL Injection Fix (PLAT-2025) — CVE-2024-31337, CVSS 9.1

The `/api/v2/search` endpoint was directly interpolating `req.query.q` into a raw SQL string. An authenticated attacker could exfiltrate arbitrary org data.

**Fix:** Replaced `db.rawSearch()` with ORM-based parameterized queries. The `rawSearch` helper is deleted entirely.

**BREAKING:** Search result ordering changed slightly. Wildcard `%` characters in query strings now treated as literals (was a security vulnerability, not a feature).

Also upgraded axios (CVE-2024-39338) and lodash (CVE-2021-23337).

## Test plan
- [x] Rate limit: verified 429 returned after burst, Retry-After header present
- [x] SQL injection: confirmed parameterized queries in EXPLAIN output
- [x] Penetration test: attempted injection payloads all rejected
- [x] Regression: search results consistent for normal queries

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2020
- Jira: PLAT-2025

title: feat!: remove deprecated v1 API endpoints and modernize JWT structure
body:
## Summary

Cleans up two long-standing deprecations announced in v2.3.0 (released 2024-01-10):
1. **Remove /api/v1/* endpoints** — migration window was 6 months; v2.5.0 is the cutoff
2. **JWT payload modernization** — remove `legacy_uid`, add `jti` for token revocation

## BREAKING CHANGES

### /api/v1/* Removal (PLAT-2026)
All v1 endpoints have been removed:
- `GET/POST /api/v1/users` → use `/api/v2/users`
- `GET/POST /api/v1/organizations` → use `/api/v2/orgs`
- `POST /api/v1/auth/login` → use `/api/v2/auth/token`
- `GET /api/v1/billing` → use `/api/v2/payments/history`
- All `/api/v1/webhooks/*` → use `/api/v2/webhooks/*`

See migration guide: `docs/migrations/v1-to-v2.md`

### JWT payload change (PLAT-2026)
- `legacy_uid` field **removed** (was deprecated since v2.0; identical to `sub`)
- `jti` claim **added** (UUID, enables server-side token revocation)
- All existing JWT tokens become invalid after deploy. **Users must re-authenticate.**

⚠️ Coordinate deploy with customer success team — a login prompt will appear for all active sessions.

## Test plan
- [x] All v1 routes return 404 after removal
- [x] JWT tokens with `legacy_uid` rejected by new middleware
- [x] New tokens contain `jti` and validate correctly
- [x] Token revocation flow works end-to-end

## Related
- Jira: PLAT-2026
